paper_id: Franco et al. - 2018 - An ontology for role playing games
input_limitations: The extracted markdown contains character encoding noise in author names and some accented words; figure content is represented only as [FIGURE].

evidence_quotes.quote: In this paper, we introduce a new, comprehensive ontology specially designed for modeling Role-Playing Games semantics.

We propose a comprehensive ontology specially designed for modeling RPG games for semantic applications, which is built on top of Unified Foundational Ontology (UFO) [5] and covers elements from Interactive Storytelling (IS).
Therefore, our model is expected to be able to cover the adventures and missions found in both tabletop and digital RPG titles.

goals.justification: The model is explicitly created to model RPG semantics and cover RPG domain concepts across tabletop and digital titles. This directly matches the Understanding goal of clarifying and representing a domain.

evidence_quotes.quote: After exporting data from our model simulating individuals found in both games to the OWL format, a total of 2,233 triplets was generated resorting to the Triple-Store Apache Jena Fuseki [27] persistent triple store.
Moreover, SPARQL queries were executed to respond to the previously enumerated competence questions.

goals.justification: The ontology was used to export data to OWL, generate triples, populate a triple store, and run SPARQL queries. This supports Intervening because the model enabled creation of implementation artifacts, though the evidence appears in evaluation rather than as the main stated purpose.

evidence_quotes.quote: This work aims to provide an improved semantic model, which is necessary to consult and discuss a given material in common for these RPG platforms.

uncertain_goals.reason: The sentence mentions discussing common material, but it does not clearly identify distinct stakeholders, interaction, explanation, or an actual communication setting.
